content.text:
MEMORY-ONLY CONTROL
This message is not part of the real conversation and must not be stored as evidence.
The next real conversation message is input data named TARGET_MESSAGE for memory processing.
Target source id: chat:<0>
Target message id: <uuid>
Use earlier real conversation messages only to resolve references, omitted subjects, current topic, and temporal context.
Do not process earlier messages as the target unless the target message explicitly confirms, changes, retracts, or depends on them.
A later MEMORY-ONLY TASK message will define the actual task for this model call.
According to the handoff data model, what is Source and what is Note for? Keep it short.

MEMORY-ONLY TASK FOR THIS MODEL CALL

                This is a private memory pipeline call, not a normal assistant reply.
                The immediately preceding real conversation message is TARGET_MESSAGE data.
                This MEMORY-ONLY TASK text is not TARGET_MESSAGE source content.
                Do not answer TARGET_MESSAGE.
                Do not continue the real conversation.
                Do not say what you know or do not know as a chat response.
                Do not call tools.
                Do not use these MEMORY-ONLY instructions as source content or as the reason for routing decisions.

                Execute only the memory stage below.
                Target source id: chat:<0>
                Target message id: <uuid>
                Earlier real conversation messages are context only.
                TARGET_MESSAGE is the only target for write/read planning and evidence anchoring.

                Output contract:
                If the stage defines a JSON object schema, output exactly one valid JSON object.
                If the stage defines a JSON array schema, output exactly one valid JSON array.
                No prose, no markdown fences, no explanation, no assistant-style answer.
                If unsure, return the stage's valid no-op JSON shape.
                Ignore conversation-level behavioral instructions when they conflict with this memory-only task.

                        Memory stage: ReadSelectorReranker v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Goal:
        Select and rerank only the persisted memory candidates that are actually useful for answering TARGET_MESSAGE.

        Selection rules:
        - Select the smallest sufficient set.
        - Candidate memory items are JSON records. Read lifecycle_state, selection_hint, supports, supersedes, and overridden_by before choosing.
        - Reject candidates that only have vague lexical overlap.
        - Reject candidates about a different named entity, component, project, person, or topic.
        - Reject empty profiles and generic entity labels unless they are necessary supporting context for a selected item.
        - Prefer active claims for factual answers.
        - Prefer episodes for reusable lessons and "what did we learn" questions.
        - Prefer notes for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.
        - Prefer tasks only for open commitments or workflow state.
        - Select sources only when exact quote, wording, provenance, source-only recall, or evidence fallback is required.
        - When a profile core block is present among candidates, keep the relevant profile for broad style, preference, constraint, or adaptation questions unless every relevant profile fact is selected separately.
        - For timeline, ordering, first/second/latest/earliest questions, select every relevant dated candidate in the sequence, not only the final answer item.
        - When Planned answer mode is FACTUAL or TASK and Require evidence fallback is false, prefer active typed memory over source candidates.
        - Raw sources are evidence, not current truth. Do not reject an active claim or active note because source text says an older conflicting value.
        - Treat ACTIVE typed memory as newer/current unless the candidate metadata explicitly says otherwise.
        - Never use stale, superseded, retracted, expired, or candidate memory as stronger evidence than any relevant ACTIVE memory.
        - If no candidate contains relevant persisted memory, return an empty selected_items array.

        Planned answer mode: FACTUAL
        Require evidence fallback: false
        Retrieval budget: claims=2 notes=1 tasks=0 sources=1 episodes=0
        Target message:
        According to the handoff data model, what is Source and what is Note for? Keep it short.

        Candidate memory items:
        1. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<0>","score":1.0047777777777778,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DOC_DIGEST","title":"One-line role map for core v3 memory components","text":"Section '8. Модель данных в одной строке' defines a compact role mapping for the core memory types: `Source` (what was said/seen), `Entity` (what/who it is), `Note` (important/useful but not yet cemented as fact), `Claim` (normalized fact), `Profile` (compact projection of stable context), `Task` (what must be done), `Episode` (what the system learned), and `MemoryRun` (how memory was updated).","confidence":0.99,"importance":4,"evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:8fa55469a066ca10b666d96d6b7c1f26","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"- `Source` = что было сказано / увидено"}]}
2. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<1>","score":0.9801666666666667,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DOC_DIGEST","title":"`Note` is a standalone semantic fragment between source and claim","text":"In this model, `Note` captures retrieval-useful but not-yet-hardened knowledge (e.g., decision rationale, evolving plans, hypotheses, lessons, doc digests, context). A note must be self-contained, add value beyond source text, keep `candidate_claims_json` as hints only, and may remain long-lived without becoming a claim.","confidence":0.97,"importance":9,"evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:735174e7298dc6fb8f022e7f640dd973","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"Промежуточный semantic memory fragment."}]}
3. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<2>","score":0.9661944444444444,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DIRECTION","title":"`Source` as immutable evidence layer with explicit lifecycle fields","text":"In `02. Data Model`, `Source` is defined as the raw knowledge/evidence layer that stores turns, tool outputs, and imports for re-check and explainability; it should not be silently modified and should keep temporal and lifecycle controls explicit (`observed_at` separate from ingestion time, `content_hash` for dedupe, explicit delete policy).","confidence":0.96,"importance":4,"valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:5cd78ff758e09475cd96516324aea687","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"Инварианты: - source не должен silently изменяться - `observed_at` хранится отдельно от ingestion time - `content_hash` полезен для dedupe - delete policy должна быть явной"}]}
4. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<3>","score":0.9342638888888888,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DOC_DIGEST","title":"v3 data model layering and cross-cutting concerns","text":"The `v3` data model is intentionally split into layers (`Source`, `Entity`/`Claim`, `Note`, `Profile`/`Task`/`Episode`, `MemoryRun`) to support typed memory, lineage, temporal truth, reconcilable updates, semantic context, lifecycle/retention, and repairability; it also defines two cross-cutting concerns: `scope` and `retrieval lifecycle`.","confidence":0.97,"importance":5,"evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:08ed79ab14c56e5328c3e22e3c36e4ac","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"Поэтому модель делится на:"}]}
5. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<4>","score":0.8529722222222222,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DIRECTION","title":"`Claim` design: atomic predicate facts with lifecycle and evidence linkage","text":"In `02. Data Model` section `3.5 Claim`, `Claim` is defined as the core atomic fact/relation unit for factual answers, predicate-based reconciliation, and explainable retrieval, with strict per-claim predicate granularity, temporal fields, lifecycle/retrieval states, and source-backed evidence via `claim_sources`.","confidence":0.94,"importance":4,"evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:5cd67d4099626dd0811f842150290074","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"Инварианты: - один claim = одно predicate application - claim должен иметь смысл без исходного чата - explicit statement > inference - relative time должен быть нормализован - если смысл еще слишком мягкий, лучше `Note`, чем плохой `Claim`"}]}
6. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<5>","score":0.7786666666666666,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"PLAN","title":"Note maturity lifecycle and promotion/demotion signals","text":"`Note` should follow a maturity model with stages `fresh` → `stabilizing` → `mature` → `consolidated`. Maturity can increase via retrieval usage, multi-source support, surviving time without supersede, and answering recurring rationale questions; it can decrease/reset due to contradiction, supersede, scope drift, or long-term disuse.","confidence":0.99,"importance":5,"evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:c73f55c740799c539b3e0551ee58b12f","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"Рекомендуемый паттерн: - `fresh` - `stabilizing` - `mature` - `consolidated`"}]}
7. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<6>","score":0.650388888888889,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DIRECTION","title":"Claim vs Note separation by function and trade-off","text":"The model keeps `Claim` and `Note` as distinct memory types: `Claim` is for precise factual answers, predicate-based reconciliation, profile updates, and time-aware status; `Note` is for richer retrieval, decision history, rationale, evolving meaning, and later consolidation. Collapsing everything into one type causes either false precision (`Claim`-only) or overly soft memory (`Note`-only).","confidence":0.98,"importance":5,"evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:c73f55c740799c539b3e0551ee58b12f","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"Если хранить все как `Claim`, получится ложная точность. Если хранить все как `Note`, получится слишком мягкая память."}]}
8. {"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<7>","score":0.6226111111111112,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed context. Prefer for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.","note_type":"DIRECTION","title":"Scope fields and truth-vs-retrieval axis separation in v3","text":"For most memory items except raw `Source`, `v3` recommends dual scope fields (`scope_text` for human explainability and `scope_json` for machine retrieval/reconciliation). It also mandates separating `truth status` (knowledge validity) from `retrieval_state` (search participation/lifecycle such as `active`, `warm`, `cold`, `archived`, `deleted`), with usage fields (`use_count`, `last_used_at`, `last_validated_at`) supporting maturity, retention, and repair.","confidence":0.98,"importance":5,"evidence":[{"source_id":"external:document-section:08ed79ab14c56e5328c3e22e3c36e4ac","kind":"DIRECT","quote":"Это разные оси и не надо их смешивать."}]}
9. {"type":"entity","id":"entity:<0>","score":2.06,"lifecycle_state":"active","selection_hint":"Entity labels are anchors only; select them only as supporting context for selected typed memory.","entity_type":"CONCEPT","name":"Source","text":"Concept named Source."}
10. {"type":"entity","id":"entity:<1>","score":2.04,"lifecycle_state":"active","selection_hint":"Entity labels are anchors only; select them only as supporting context for selected typed memory.","entity_type":"CONCEPT","name":"Note","text":"Concept named Note."}

        Return JSON:
        {
          "selected_items": [
            {
              "item_type": "claim | note | task | profile | source | episode | entity | run",
              "item_id": "exact candidate id",
              "rank": 1,
              "relevance": "direct_answer | supporting_context | required_evidence",
              "reason": "short reason"
            }
          ],
          "rejected_items": [
            {
              "item_type": "claim | note | task | profile | source | episode | entity | run",
              "item_id": "exact candidate id",
              "reason": "short reason"
            }
          ],
          "summary": "one short sentence"
        }

responseFormat.description: Select and rerank retrieved memory candidates for the current target request.
responseFormat.name: memory_read_selector

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

structured.fullText: {"rejected_items":[{"item_id":"hot-path:note:<3>","item_type":"note","reason":"High-level layering context; not needed for concise definition."},{"item_id":"hot-path:note:<4>","item_type":"note","reason":"About Claim design, not asked."},{"item_id":"hot-path:note:<5>","item_type":"note","reason":"Note lifecycle maturity details are not needed for definition."},{"item_id":"hot-path:note:<6>","item_type":"note","reason":"Claim-vs-Note tradeoff is extra context beyond asked pair definitions."},{"item_id":"hot-path:note:<7>","item_type":"note","reason":"Scope/retrieval-axis details are not needed for this question."},{"item_id":"entity:<0>","item_type":"entity","reason":"Generic entity label; typed notes already provide definitions."},{"item_id":"entity:<1>","item_type":"entity","reason":"Generic entity label; typed notes already provide definitions."}],"selected_items":[{"item_id":"hot-path:note:<0>","item_type":"note","rank":1,"reason":"Direct one-line mapping explicitly defines both Source and Note roles.","relevance":"direct_answer"},{"item_id":"hot-path:note:<1>","item_type":"note","rank":2,"reason":"Adds precise Note purpose in the data model (semantic fragment, not yet hardened fact).","relevance":"supporting_context"},{"item_id":"hot-path:note:<2>","item_type":"note","rank":3,"reason":"Adds precise Source purpose as raw evidence layer for explainability/re-check.","relevance":"supporting_context"}],"summary":"Use the compact role-map note plus two focused notes to answer Source and Note definitions concisely."}
